parentQuestion: A patient with advanced multiple sclerosis reports she must watch her feet const...

scaffolds.q: Proprioception allows you to know where your body parts are without looking.
scaffolds.explain: Proprioceptors in muscles, tendons, and joints continuously send position and movement data to the brain. You don't think about it — it's a silent background feed. Dive into this:: lose it, and you'll need your eyes to tell you where your hand is. The horror of becoming lost in your own body.

scaffolds.q: Multiple sclerosis damages the myelin sheath around nerve axons.
scaffolds.explain: MS is a demyelinating disease — the immune system attacks myelin sheaths, slowing or blocking signal transmission. Demyelination affects ANY myelinated pathway: motor, sensory, visual. See this through: now: signals from proprioceptors travel on large, heavily myelinated fibers. Perfect targets for MS. The fastest highways, the first to fail.
